item.title: Actual footage of 2490 on the hunt
(no title)
item.content:encoded:
Targets uniformly report SCP-2490 either moving towards them without moving its legs (with its arms outstretched) or…

Is no one going to talk about the fact that it aggressively t-poses towards people??
Oh hey! Glad to see this finally broke the 100 barrier!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm super confused by some of these other comments. On its own, sure, it might approach slightly 'generic monster wut stalks yu and noms ur brainz'. But…that's wildly not the point. The point is hinted at in the first sentence of the description, and confirmed harder and harder the farther you get as you go through the obscured documents.

NOBODY KNOWS WHAT THIS IS. They all think it belongs to someone else, which is why the GoI tie-ins are both present and ABSOLUTELY NECESSARY. Something hunting through every organization that interacts with these anomalous objects to retrieve them for unknown purposes? That's huge, and just incredibly, quietly sinister. It's phenomenal, and the tie-ins and references are all absolutely spot-on.

The only thing I'd even consider changing would be maybe to hint at a possible inclusion of MTF Eta-10("See No Evil") to test if they can detect it with their weird indirect viewing stuff, but that's hardly necessary and might just be due to me really liking the MTFs and wanting them to show up more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Embarrassingly late response again - but doing that would ruin the reveal and the stinger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I'd consider changing

SCP-2490 refers to a modified human being, believed to be Chaos Insurgency Special Operative Alpha-19, that has stalked at least ██ Foundation employees since 19██.

to

SCP-2490 refers to a modified human being, believed to be Chaos Insurgency Special Operative Alpha-19, of unknown origin, that has stalked at least ██ Foundation employees since 19██.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it as "just another creepy stalker monster" with the twist that it's an agent of an existing organization and works in service of their goals rather than purely acting strange and malevolent on its own terms. Then I found out that even the CI doesn't know what this thing's deal is, and is only pointing it at their enemies to keep it off their backs. That simultaneously returned the trope to its roots (it really is vaguely malevolent on its own terms and not fully aligned with the CI's interests) and twisted it even further (the CI has a leash on it, but they don't know how strong their hold is or what they should or shouldn't do next).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Iiiii… I mean, it's creepy! I get what it does, and it's creepy, but somehow I can't upvote. Something is stopping me. Possibly that it's just simple and done. I could easily see this as a generic monster in a horror video game, and that's not a good thing. Sure, the interview adds and the CI thing adds, but it doesn't add enough to stop it from being "thing what looks scary and stalks you". As others have said, very Slender.

I would downvote, but…. that was an extremely well written interview, and I can't downvote something with that kind of dialogue. I definitely saw the "he dies at the end" coming, as soon as the interview started, but that's my only complaint about the interview.

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to say this is downright terrifying, and it's been a while since I've read a skip that makes me look over my shoulder! Sometimes I forget there's horror on this site, you know? :P

That said:

Surveillance footage shows no abnormalities besides a consistent 0.5 second period of static, during which time the SCP will disappear. Attempts to watch anomalous objects in-person result in brief periods of vision failure (usually through malfunctioning lights, human error, or optical instrument failure) during which time the SCP will disappear.

This is Series III, so based on current site standards, it's a little odd that it's referred to as "the SCP" here rather than "the object", etc. It makes sense to have it called that in the dialogue, but not the actual article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in love with this. The fact that this guy was once a man is creepier. This is why I think the newer ones are the scariest. Thanks so much for this masterpiece.

Huh, the guy wasn't a man, but there are more of them out there…

that's even MORE CREEPY!

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded: This seems like it was inspired by Slender man from SLENDER, which felt weird(the game!). After reading this…I am so fucking creeped out! I gave this an up vote for the two simple reasons: the creepy eyes with nothing else AND Mr.Feldman's interview…Wow. But there is one catch that would have led me to a down vote: the description not being too clear…Seems like my SCP seems the same, but who cares! This deserves an up vote! I even favourited this on my Offline SCP Database app.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's two methods of locomotion are the two creepiest methods I can imagine, that alone was almost worth the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this, but didn't quite get the ending. Very scary. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad but needs work for an upvote. Not worth a downvote at all though.

The entity needs to be better defined, or if you want to keep it vague maybe a cloak, open at the front to show that its a mannequin (or maybe its kept closed but the victim just knows its a mannequin). Height needs to be included definitely. It sounds like its going to take a lot of research to puzzle out all the GOI references. I like puzzles but if it turns out to be more of a slender man type entity I'll be a little disappointed >.<

The brain sucking is interesting, implying the knowledge is used in the theft of the items. The number and style of the SCP obviously still need to be confirmed but a horde of these little buggers than can change size would be a nightmare :D

Be interested to see where its going but I dont really know enough to advise further. Looks good though, well written documents ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's not bad, but I'm gonna have to downvote:

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-2490 refers to a modified human being, believed to be Chaos Insurgency Special Operative Alpha-19

  • I personally don't like excessive references to GOIs, and this scip seems to ride on the GOC stuff in the addenda. That's just my opinion, though.

SCP-2490 will pursue the target for anywhere between several hours and several months, often showing itself to the target from a range before fleeing. As a result of this behavior, targets usually express lack of sleep, extreme paranoia, introversion, and a phobia of mannequins.

  • "Pathological fear of mannequins" or "automatonophobia" (fear of anything that falsely represents a sentient being) sound more clinical. I don't know if your phrasing is incorrect or if it just sounds weird to me. "Aversion to mannequins" would work, too.
  • We have a lot of scips that stalk, and a lot of mannequins- I'm strongly reminded of Sleep Killer and The Maybe-There Monsters. both of which are creepier than this.

targets are found unconscious, with puncture marks in their skull. MRI scanning reveals that brain tissue from the cerebellum, hippocampus, cerebral cortex, and random sections of the frontal, parietal, and temporal lobes has been removed and replaced with a fluid similar to SCP-████'s secretions.

  • Loaves of the brain, neato.
  • vampiring brains out is pretty cool. The reference to SCP-blackbox's secretions replacing the brain strikes me as unnecessary, like it's not adding anything to the scip- unless i'm missing something. SCP's are usually supposed to be able to stand alone, and referencing other scips (real or not) can weaken your article. Same goes for GOIs and stuff, usually.

Following an attack, a random SCP that the target previously worked with will disappear

  • Ok, cool, it's eating the headpudding and then using the stolen memories to steal itself some scips? that's… kind of a big deal.

SCP-2490's head is composed of a single, oblong beige capsule, on which two eyes have been painted and no other facial features are present. Its body consists of multiple white, jointed segments. SCP-2490 possesses claws that appear to be grafted to its hand pods. Based on target testimony, however, most observers perceive it as an ordinary human being.

  • yes, but how tall is it? does it have four legs? where is its head? is it humanoid? whaaaat are "hand-pods"?

possesses claws that appear to be grafted to its hand pods

  • Grafted, like, surgically? are there seams or welds or something? Have they actually been added to the skip, or do they just "appear" to?

Overall, it's well-written but similar to other scips and relies too heavily on things that aren't itself. So, uh, do with that what you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good writing, but it ultimately might not be my thing at the end of the day. Feels too much like generic monster creepypasta that stalks you (albeit with a nice twist on it) and the description you gave of the monster made me imagine it to look too absurd to be scary (from how I pictured it anyway). The Chaos Insurgency file, while alright, seems to be a bit too silly for my tastes (specifically at "STEP 60/034"; i know this is the Insurgency, but holy shit that is melodramatic and OTT for my liking).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sufficiently freaked out. +1. Also, I'm now imagining playing a modified version of Slender against this thing.

item.title: 5p00ky dr34m5
item.content:encoded:
One night after playing video games I got into bed and suddenly imagined a mannequin staring at me from the door in the middle of the night.
Yeah.

First real Keter, too!

Thanks to Eskobar, Cimmerian, TwistedGears, and a few others whom I can't recall for helping me craft this twisted mannequin.

See more of my work on my author page!
